questions.question: What is the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of 5 and 7?
questions.explanation: Since 5 and 7 are prime numbers, they are coprime. The LCM of coprime numbers is simply their product: $5 \times 7 = 35$.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 12 and 18?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 12 are 12, 24, 36, 48... Multiples of 18 are 18, 36, 54... The smallest common multiple is 36. 6 is the GCD, not the LCM.

questions.question: If you know that $\text{GCD}(a, b) = 4$ and $a \times b = 96$, what is the value of $\text{LCM}(a, b)$?
questions.explanation: Using the relation $\text{LCM}(a, b) \times \text{GCD}(a, b) = a \times b$, we have $\text{LCM} \times 4 = 96$. Therefore, $\text{LCM} = 96 \div 4 = 24$.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct method for finding the LCM using prime factorization?
options: Multiply the lowest powers of shared prime factors.
Multiply the highest powers of all prime factors present in either number.
Divide the product of the numbers by their sum.
questions.explanation: To find the LCM, you identify all prime bases present in either factorization and multiply the highest exponents of each base.

questions.question: A digital sign flashes Message A every 8 seconds and Message B every 12 seconds. If they flash together now, after how many seconds will they next flash at the same time?
questions.explanation: We need to find the LCM of 8 and 12. Multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24, 32... Multiples of 12: 12, 24, 36... The LCM is 24 seconds.
